FYI, cat is okay 👍

My wife came home and saw me covered in blood, trying to clean blood off the floor, and my daughter excitedly telling her about it. I just looked at her and said I needed help, and she didn't question anything, just got the mop and started helping clean up.

A lot was learned.

I was completely covered in cat blood, it was all over the floor, animals going crazy over it, my 3yo daughter was amused and would try to walk around it for fun... She watched the whole thing and was completely unaffected... Probably because she didn't really understand it.

He was fine for a moment, but after 30 seconds he started leaking blood everywhere. I chased him around the house as he shook his paw just flicking blood every which way. I finally catch him and after a couple of attempts of him running away was able to reapply the bandage.

Last night I picked up my cat from the emergency vet (he swiped one of my dogs pain meds from his bowl which we found out is extremely toxic), total vet bill was $4000. He seems to be okay, but when he got home the vet said to unwrap his IV bandage. That was a mistake - TW: Blood

Guys sorry I've been pretty unresponsive. A lot of life stuff has come up to manage and any free time is immediately sunk into redoing the MogTalk website to try to have phase 1 beta ready by the time the world race starts.

I keep thinking "what if I program myself into a hole" - but honestly it is probably more important to make something, and figure it out later instead of freezing up on making the "best" decision.

Even if you have to fix something, at least you have something in the first place.

Programming is hard. I write some code, feel good about it, then while writing some more code realize what I wrote before doesn't work anymore and have to rewrite it. It's such a vicious loop. Every big progress step turns into a small step.

But at least there's steps...

There are plenty of creators I know who do it very well and I'm extremely proud of them, but there are plenty I see struggling with it. Stabilize your needs so your wants are actually what you want.

End game content creation where money is the only goal hurts everyone.

I had to make a choice a year ago because financially content creation couldn't support what I wanted for my family and myself.

I have so much less room for content creation now, but I know when I do it - I'm making what I want, and not what I need to survive.

Everyone needs to make money, and to create content as a career requires knowing how to properly monetize creativity.

If you can't figure that out, you may lose all creativity and find yourself in a depressing inescapable spiral of chasing the bag.

Few do it well, I couldn't.
I hate when I type a long post and find the typo only after I send it..

There are plenty of creators I know who do it very well and I'm very proud of them. 

But there are plenty I see struggling with it. Stabilize your needs so your wants are actually what you want.

Most of the people who give me energy and help me stay positive you'll never know. There's a lot of really awesome people and most of them aren't on social media front page.

Without them, I would have stopped long ago. I'm sure you have people like that too, appreciate them.

I expect more info to come to adjust the ranking, but it's the best we can do at the moment. Feel free to advise if something seems off. The lodestone leaderboard wasn't built for first clears, but instead for scoring - so it will change over time. For now it's useful though.

The reason players may not show on the lodestone leaderboard is due to points not counting while doing world traveling. This could be the case for other clears we don't know about (will update if we find out)

If someone cleared already, we didn't include their second clear.

Working w/ @sig9tv we've compiled the likely first 100 solo clears for PT. 

It's based off of the official leaderboard, however due to world travel not everyone shows on there. The top two have video evidence of getting the achievement.

https://t.co/J2lwwwYLDi
Just a heads up, I'll be updating leaderboards tonight - lots of day job work to catch up with.

I do want to of course give an shout out again to @TeamLucrezia.

It was extremely exciting to see them come together for an in person race event. They've put a lot of work to grow over the years and it's inspiring seeing that hard work pay off. 

Thanks for letting us watch.

Covering Pilgrim's Traverse in a World Race format was an experimental effort by our team. It turned out to be the most difficult one yet, but I'm glad we did it & as a community found a way to come together again. Learned lots & hopefully more experimental races/events to come!
Never been a harder race to track. We will update as we find more information if it comes up. https://t.co/v5vIlbwseq
I take it back, it was a learning experience https://t.co/q5yrE5uSL2

We will keep trying to do our best, this is the first time trying to do deep dungeon and didn't expect the Q40 element to be what it is.

Sorry for the messy tracking.

Spreading this across all the people trying to clear - it's near impossible to manually track this. 

This is why public combat logs or an official API of some sort could make races possible.

Problems with tracking:

Q40 - Multiple boss health pools means microanalyzing bars on streams with no clear check point.

Solo - Multiple checkpoints require constantly finding out if the player is fresh prog or check pointed.

Resources - we will have 1 tracker most of the time
It was a mistake to track both solo and q40

We are currently figuring out how to track the Q40 fight... two different health pools make it hard to track via streams.

Might get mechanic based tracking, but that's a bit of work too. Hang with us.

The deep dungeon community is one of the key pillars in the FFXIV community. They help tons of players extend gameplay for a MMO we all love.

I'm extremely glad they are getting new content tomorrow & very excited to see them all tackle it.

Good luck and have fun everyone!
